message:
test(channel): add audit regression tests for broadcast and mpsc channels

Add comprehensive audit tests (SapphireHill, 2026-02-15) covering
edge cases and invariants in the broadcast and mpsc channel modules.

Broadcast channel tests (src/channel/broadcast.rs):
- total_sent_advances_even_when_buffer_evicts: verify total_sent is a
  monotonic sequence number that advances regardless of ring buffer
  eviction, and that the buffer retains only the most recent N entries
- subscribe_from_lagged_position_gets_only_future: new subscribers
  positioned at total_sent only see messages sent after subscription
- multiple_receivers_independent_lag: each receiver tracks its own
  read cursor; one advancing does not affect another's lag detection
- permit_send_returns_zero_after_all_receivers_drop: SendPermit::send
  correctly returns 0 and leaves total_sent/buffer unchanged when no
  receivers exist
- capacity_one_overwrites_correctly: capacity=1 ring buffer evicts
  on every send, receiver detects exact lag count

MPSC channel tests (src/channel/mpsc.rs):
- send_evict_oldest_wakes_receiver: send_evict_oldest properly wakes
  a pending receiver after evicting the oldest buffered message
- weak_sender_upgrade_increments_sender_count: WeakSender::upgrade
  correctly increments sender_count; channel stays open until all
  strong senders drop
- capacity_invariant_across_reserve_send_abort: used_slots invariant
  holds correctly when mixing reserve, send, and abort operations
- try_reserve_respects_fifo_over_capacity: try_reserve returns Full
  when waiters are queued even if raw capacity is available, ensuring
  FIFO fairness for pending reserve futures
- send_evict_oldest_disconnected_after_receiver_drop: send_evict_oldest
  returns Disconnected with the value when the receiver is dropped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
